titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Edward O'Farrell to William Brace, 14 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter was written by Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926) referring to a list of German civilian Prisoners of War. According to the letter, a proposal has been made to repatriate the German civilians in exchange for British subjects of the same class detained in Germany.
The letter was addressed to the Under Secretary of State for Home Affairs, William Brace (1865 - 1947).

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5618/12046

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
14th July 1916. Sir, With reference to your letter of the 5th
instant forwarding a list of German civilian
Prisoners of War, previously resident in Ireland,
whom it is proposed to repatriate in exchange for
British subjects of the same class at present
detained in Germany, I am directed by the Lords
Justices to transmit, for submission to the
Secretary of State, the accompanying police re-
ports respecting the aliens name. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, EO'Farrell original The Under Secretary of State,
Home Office,
London, S.W.
